title: All About Falls: Why They Happen, Predicting Them, and What to Do
description: This week I've created three short videos that will show you why falls happen, how to predict them, and how to manage a fall so neither you nor your loved one is injured.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: A Toolbox for Creating Meaningful Moments with Your Loved One
description: Also in this edition: Perfect caregiver posture for transfers. Fix your posture in under 30 seconds to protect your back.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: Your biggest caregiving weakness is hiding something
description: This week, my mom ended up in the hospital. What happened there reminded me of something family caregivers don't hear enough.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: Dementia Prevention, Prediction, and Detection: What Have We Learned?
description: Also in this edition: information overload and what we can do today about dementia.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: Breakthrough Reveals Gene Driving Most of Dementia
description: Also in this edition: Video - How to Safely Use Your Muscles for Transfers
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: Research Roundup: Top Dementia Advances of 2025
description: Also in this edition: how to cope with repetition from a loved one with dementia.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: A Dementia Newsletter First! (oops)
description: Last week was the first time I missed pushing out an edition of the Newsletter. Why? Because I was attending an intense industry Think Tank in Austin, TX and couldn't break away for even a quick update. However, this week I'm telling you how that conference will change everything.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: Stick 'Em Up! Post-It Power and Dementia
description: When memory starts to fade, many adults make the most of their time by finding the memory cues that work for them.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: How to Validate What You Don't Understand
description: Despite the fact that the word “validation” has hit the mainstream in the American culture of active listening practices, I don’t feel like it’s particularly well-understood.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: Asker vs Guesser Families: Dementia Does Make Guessers of Us All
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: 5 Things to Check First—Understanding Dementia Behaviors
description: Understanding behaviors as “responsive” is a critical first step. Behaviors don’t simply spring into the world on their own. There is always a cause, and behaviors are an effect, a response.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: Goldilocks and the Three Caregivers
description: Also in this edition: the results from the Walk to End Alzheimer's!
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: Beyond “lift with your legs”: the secret mechanics of safe transfers
description: A special edition of the Newsletter devoted to transfer safety. Includes 4 short videos (0:20–1:00) to reduce injury risk now.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: What to Do When There's Nothing You Can Do
description: My mom was hospitalized twice this weekend, once after a fall and then again with pneumonia. She was largely unresponsive for about 24 hours. I wanted to do something, help in some way, fix the situation... That’s what an expert would know how to do, right?
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: Labor Day Edition: Work Cues That Soothe Your Loved One
description: Because work has such a tremendous weight for most of us, I wonder why it’s so difficult to find information about dementia activities that leverage an interest in work.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: Never Have I Ever...Put My Loved One In a Nursing Home
description: What happens when a promise to never place a loved one in a nursing home conflicts with what's best for everyone involved.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: Mom's Memory Care Move: Before and After
description: Also in this edition: My tips for getting through a big change for someone who has dementia, especially a move to memory care.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: How One Family Lost Thousands to an Unlicensed Care Scam
description: "What if I'm making the wrong decision for my loved one?" Has there ever been a more haunting and persistent question for people who care for a loved one with dementia?
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: 8 Red Flags that Signal Financial Abuse
description: There are signs you may observe that should put you on your guard when helping your loved one. These may not be comfortable conversations, but I can tell you from my first-hand experience that they are critical.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: Why Independence Day Hit Different This Year
description: As fireworks lit up the sky last night, I found myself thinking about freedom in new ways, ways I didn’t understand before becoming a caregiver. Celebrations of liberty feel different when you're helping someone button their shirt or choose what to have for breakfast.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: I Love My Readers, So Can We Get Engaged?
description: Inside you'll get my top picks for books about engaging loved ones with dementia along with other ideas for creating a life of thriving!
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: Is a Monster Hiding under Your Loved One's Bed?
description: I love a good horror movie. I've usually got some terrible B-movie going on in the background for fun, but I realized lately that they've also taught me how to better understand my mom's dementia paranoia and fear.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: Fun Fact: Your Reality Isn't True Either
description: As we dig deeper into this universe of ours, we’re finding that our senses have misled us on some of our most fundamental assumptions. And yet, we think our reality is correct while our loved ones' realities are incorrect because they have dementia.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: "If This Isn't Nice, What Is?" Kurt Vonnegut's Lessons for Caregivers
description: This edition draws on the wisdom of one of my favorite authors, Kurt Vonnegut, and the words of hope we can take from him as caregivers.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: Parlez-vous Dementia? How French Class Taught Me About Dementia and Loneliness
description: In this edition we explore communication beyond words. When our loved one with dementia struggles with verbal communication, what can we do to meet their needs and preserve a loving and engaged relationship?
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: There are two ways to say "no": one kills and one heals
description: Also in this edition: find the perfect father's day gift for a loved one with dementia, and pick up your invitation to the "revolution"!
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: Be an A+ 🥇🏆 Caregiver: Get Lazier ASAP!
description: There’s a principle called the 85% rule that came from analyzing the performance of legendary track star Carl Lewis. Find out how it relates to our caregiving in this edition.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: Did you invite disaster to today's party? Put out the welcome mat!
description: Today is the LAST DAY to get your 50% off subscriber's discount at elumenEd! Act now to get the savings.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: 💥Transform Dementia Dining Now!
description: This edition is all about creating an easy and exciting dining experience. There are links to some exceptional articles about dementia dining from around the web, and a special guest column by our friends from the Tapestry Newsletter!
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: 🧭 Dementia Levels of Care Explained: Make Confident Decisions 🧭
description: Also in this edition: level of care and funding resources from the National Institute on Aging; the future and evolution of senior care; how to talk about the most difficult thing with your loved one.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: 🧘 Zen and the Art of Caregiver Maintenance 🪷
description: Also in this edition: Meet Auguste Deter, the first person diagnosed with Alzheimer's disease; get a free consultation (if you hurry); do you know what compassion REALLY means?
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: She Asked, ‘Are You Ben?’—Here’s What I Said
description: Also in this edition: resources for being a financial caregiver, a clever way to use reminiscence therapy to manage behavior, and the touching perspective one person used to enjoy even the repetitious stories of their loved one.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: Surviving The Unexpected Swing: Responding to Physical Aggression
description: What can you do when de-escalation has failed and physical violence is happening? How can you protect yourself without hurting them? Find out in today's newsletter.
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: Why your loved one resists care, and what to do about it
description: Also in this edition: AI in caregiving; identifying dementia before middle age; know when it's time for home care and what to expect; coping with finances as a Sandwich Generation caregiver
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd

title: 5 Things Learning Greek Taught Me About My Mom's Agitation
description: Also in this edition: the latest news about dementia prediction, prevention, and research; the YouTube chronicles of a couple who are battling early-onset dementia together; video discussions about dementia from four people who are living with it; and much more!
author: The Dementia Newsletter, by elumenEd
